Mentions Timeline (5 articles)
3302-08-11
Freelance Report Buckyball Racing Club Salutes Heroes of Bast
The Buckyball Racing Club is hosting a tribute race at Hart Station in the Bast system to honour the independent pilots who helped discover and distribute the Ceremonial Heike Tea cure for the Cerberus Plague. The event includes a scramble around medicine-production facilities and a speed run to Brunel City in Heike.
3301-08-06
Fight Against Cerberus Plague Gains Ground
The fight against the Cerberus Plague is gaining ground as medical teams deliver antidotes to affected systems. The plague has been eradicated from Dajoar, Bastes, Maheou Ti, Eleu, Ochoeng, 20 Ophiuchi, Una, Santjalan, BD-02 4304, Chongguls, Xamentii, and G 139-50. Efforts continue to help those still suffering from the disease.
3301-07-29
Cerberus Plague Breakthrough
Medical personnel at Hart Station praised 765 independent pilots who delivered medicines to the Bast system. A potential breakthrough was discovered when patients given Ceremonial Heike Tea showed improvement. Chief Medical Officer Carl Simmons of Newton Dock confirmed the tea contains ingredients that can cure the pathogen, though it is rare and only found in Heike. Gathering sufficient quantities will be challenging.
3301-07-14
Reports of the Cerberus Plague beyond BD-02 4304
Chief Medical Officer Carl Simmons confirmed isolated Cerberus Plague incidents in Dajoar, Eleu, and 20 Ophiuchi, believed to be travellers from BD-02 4304 before restrictions were imposed. Three containment operations have been initiated. Simmons called on independent pilots to help prevent full outbreaks. A technician confirmed the name 'Cerberus Plague' comes from the organism's unusual three-pronged structure.
3301-06-30
Infection Running Rampant in BD 02 4304
A devastating outbreak aboard Newton Dock in BD-02 4304 has closed hundreds of businesses and quarantined thousands. The illness of unknown origin is spreading across the region with reports from surrounding systems suggesting wider contamination. Chief Medical Officer Carl Simmons warned the outbreak is spreading at an alarming rate and advised residents to stay home while travellers avoid the area. Almost 27 million residents are now at risk from disease, famine or both. Symptoms include vomiting blood, excessive sweating, sores and intense dizziness.
